sections.title: Business Requirements
sections.purpose: Define why the system should exist and how success is measured.

questions.prompt: What problem are you trying to solve?
What is the main goal of this system?
Who are the target users?
What value will this product provide to users?
How will success be measured (KPIs)?
What are users currently doing instead of this system?
What are the biggest pain points in the current process?
Is this a new product or replacing an existing one?

sections.title: Users and Roles
sections.purpose: Identify actor types, permissions, and role behavior.

questions.prompt: What types of users will use the system?
What permissions does each role have?
Can users have multiple roles?
What actions can each role perform?
Are there admin-level controls?

sections.title: Functional Requirements
sections.purpose: Define what users should be able to do in the system.
sections.agent_guidance: Do not ask what features do you want. Ask what should the user be able to do.

questions.prompt: What actions can a user perform in the system?
What is the main workflow from start to finish?
What happens after a user signs up?
What are the key operations (create, update, delete, and related actions)?
Describe the main user journey step by step.
What happens if something fails?
What are the alternative flows?
What happens if the user enters invalid data?
What if the system is unavailable?
What if a process is interrupted?

sections.title: Non-Functional Requirements
sections.purpose: Capture quality attributes such as performance, security, and reliability.

questions.prompt: How many users do you expect?
Should the system support real-time updates?
What is acceptable response time?
Should the system handle growth over time?
What is the expected traffic peak?
Does the system require authentication?
What type of authentication is required (email/password, OAuth, other)?
Is there sensitive data such as payments or personal data?
Should the system be highly available?
Is downtime acceptable?
Should it be mobile-friendly?
Are there accessibility requirements?

sections.title: Integrations
sections.purpose: Identify third-party systems and integration needs.

questions.prompt: Does the system integrate with external services?
Does it need payment gateway integration?
Does it need email or SMS providers?
Are there third-party APIs to consume or expose?

sections.title: Constraints
sections.purpose: Capture limits that affect delivery and implementation.

questions.prompt: What is the deadline?
Are there required technologies?
Are there budget limitations?
Are there regulatory constraints?

sections.title: Technical Preferences
sections.purpose: Capture optional stack and hosting preferences.

questions.prompt: What is the preferred backend technology?
What is the preferred database?
What is the hosting preference?

sections.title: Data and Entities
sections.purpose: Define domain entities, fields, and relationships.

questions.prompt: What are the main entities (for example users, orders, courses)?
What data should each entity store?
How are these entities related?

sections.title: Validation Questions
sections.purpose: Validate assumptions and identify unresolved requirements.

questions.prompt: Is there anything unclear or not defined yet?
Are there any assumptions we should confirm?
What is the most critical feature?
